name: Mount Pinatubo
eruptionHistory: The June 1991 eruption was the second largest of the 20th century (VEI 6). It ejected ~10 km³ of material and lowered global temperatures by 0.5°C for a year.
description: Mount Pinatubo's cataclysmic 1991 eruption was one of the largest and most well-studied eruptions of the 20th century. The eruption produced massive pyroclastic flows and a global climate impact.
